authority: local TaxKit documentation writes and read-only local inspection only

sources.observation: Local release checks do not authorize or prove publication; provider, registry, publication, release, and deployment operations require named authority, a receipt, rollback or revocation, and readback.

Public docs are a separate product surface; draft and published have accepted local lifecycle semantics without external-availability claims, and future sanitized release receipts belong under docs/evidence/releases/**.
HGI-207 and HGI-202 are completed locally; HGI-208 is next, and no local policy claims publication, registry, deployment, credentials, or external-consumer behavior.

HGI-202 is locally complete after accepted HGI-207 choices; HGI-208 is next and release, publication, registry, deployment, provider, credential, and evidence movement still require separately approved authority and target runbooks.
The authored navigation status is draft; all 61 tracked public MDX files also declare status draft.
The navigation schema accepts a string status and does not define the semantic consequence of draft.
Changesets manage private package versions; future npm publication is conditional on later package readiness and removing private flags.

release:check records command outcomes and runs local package-owned gates, but it does not define retained candidate artifacts, log redaction, evidence location, or external publication/deployment proof.
The machine-readable owner policy represents draft as authored-candidate and published as accepted-current; acceptanceRecords is empty while all 61 MDX pages and navigation remain draft.
The decoded owner-policy schema requires accepted public lifecycle semantics and an exact accepted page-level JSON record with targetPath, accepted state, owner, and observedAt.
The checker requires each published MDX or navigation path to have a unique, addressable, schema-valid accepted record bound to that exact path, without making an external-state assertion.

limitations: This receipt is a local source inventory, not public-site, registry, deployment, provider, or external-consumer readback.
An empty retained-candidate directory inventory does not prove that no candidate or release artifact exists outside the inspected repository paths.
nonClaims: No public MDX, navigation, release artifact, evidence, runbook, provider, registry, publication, or deployment state was changed.
No maintainer approval or external authority was inferred from the inspected files.
